task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — Q2 Budget vs. Actuals
CONFIDENTIAL — Finance use only. DO NOT DISTRIBUTE.
Prepared by: NovaBridge Finance | Date: 2026-07-07

Category,Q2 Budget ($),Q2 Actual ($),Variance ($),Notes
Engineering (internal),180000,194200,+14200,Overrun: unplanned sprint added in May
Subcontractors,45000,51800,+6800,GeoSync integration scoping work
Cloud infrastructure,28000,31500,+3500,Load testing environment spun up early
QA and testing,22000,20100,-1900,Under budget; QA resource joined 2 weeks late
Travel and client meetings,12000,9400,-2600,Two site visits deferred to Q3
Contingency reserve,15000,15000,0,Untouched
TOTAL,302000,322000,+20000,

Projected additional Q3 overruns:
- GeoSync license (if not renewed before expiry): $12,000
- UAT extension (1 additional week): $15,000
- Estimated total H2 projected overrun: $47,000

Note: Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group.
CFO review scheduled: 2026-07-18
Action required: Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO by 2026-07-20.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===

Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Sprint 14 Task Assignments (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18)

Task ID  | Description                               | Assignee          | Points | Due Date   | Status

HEL-242  | Dashboard: chart rendering module         | Tomas Rivera      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | In Progress
HEL-243  | Data ingestion: date format normalization | Amir Hassan       | 5      | 2026-07-15 | Blocked
HEL-244  | Integration test suite: auth flows        | Kenji Watanabe    | 3      | 2026-07-14 | Done
HEL-245  | Integration test suite: data pipeline     | Kenji Watanabe    | 5      | 2026-07-18 | In Progress

input.content:
NovaBridge Project Health Briefing — Project Helios / Meridian Group
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Project Helios remains broadly ON TRACK, but the delivery path is tightening as several July dependencies converge ahead of the confirmed Meridian product demo on 2026-07-25. Core platform work is progressing: the API gateway is complete, the data ingestion module is approximately 85% complete, the client dashboard is approximately 60% complete, and integration testing is approximately 40% complete. However, multiple Q2 carryover items remain overdue, including integration test suite setup, UAT environment provisioning, and the performance benchmarking report.

The most urgent delivery risks are Meridian source data quality, unresolved GeoSync licensing, dashboard/ingestion performance, and UAT readiness. Leadership attention is recommended on dependency escalation with Meridian, budget overrun management, and ensuring accountable owners have the support needed to hit July commitments.

CURRENT PROGRESS

1. Delivery Status
- Overall status: ON TRACK with minor but growing risks.
- API gateway: 100% complete and delivered.
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion is 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard v1: 60% complete; target completion is 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: 40% complete; target sign-off is 2026-07-25.
- Next major milestone: Integration Test Sign-off on 2026-07-25.

2. Q2 Deliverables Carried Into Q3
Completed:
- Requirements documentation v1.0.
- System architecture design.
- API gateway implementation.
- Development environment setup.
- Data schema finalization.

In progress:
- Data ingestion module, target 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard v1, target 2026-07-22.

Overdue:
- Integration test suite setup, originally due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20.
- UAT environment provisioning, originally due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access.
- Performance benchmarking report, originally due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor.

3. Sprint 14 Execution
Sprint 14 runs from 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18. Current task status:
- HEL-241 Dashboard data table component: In Progress; due 2026-07-14.
- HEL-242 Dashboard chart rendering module: In Progress; due 2026-07-16.
- HEL-243 Data ingestion date format normalization: Blocked; due 2026-07-15.
- HEL-244 Integration test suite auth flows: Done.
- HEL-245 Integration test suite data pipeline: In Progress; due 2026-07-18.
- HEL-246 UAT environment server provisioning: At Risk; due 2026-07-16.
- HEL-247 Dashboard load performance benchmark: Not Started; due 2026-07-18.
- HEL-248 GeoSync license renewal coordination: In Progress; due 2026-07-14.

4. Testing Health
Sprint 14 testing is partially complete:
- Authentication flows: 4/4 passed.
- Data pipeline batch ingestion: 5/7 passed, with 2 open failures.
- Dashboard suite: 1/4 complete; chart rendering and load benchmark remain incomplete or not run.
- Overall sprint testing: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 failed, 3 not yet run.

Open high-severity defects:
- HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format. This affects approximately 40% of Meridian source data. Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending clean sample dataset from Meridian, due 2026-07-15.
- HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion over approximately 200k records causes memory overflow; pipeline crashes around the 210k record mark. Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

KEY RISKS

1. Meridian Source Data Quality — HIGH / HIGH / Score 9
- Status: Active.
- Issue: Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats, including ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY. Current defect HEL-D-014 confirms MM/DD/YYYY date normalization failure.
- Impact: Blocks completion of ingestion normalization and threatens the 2026-07-25 live data demo.
- Mitigation: Meridian committed to provide a clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15. Amir Hassan owns mitigation.
- Leadership view: This is the highest-priority delivery dependency and should be tracked daily until resolved.

2. GeoSync License Renewal — MEDIUM / HIGH / Score 6
- Status: Active; legal review in progress.
- Issue: Third-party mapping library GeoSync v2.1 license renewal is pending, with target resolution on 2026-07-14.
- Impact: If unresolved, it may compromise demo readiness and create additional licensing cost exposure.
- Mitigation owner: Raj Patel, with legal support.
- Leadership view: Ensure Legal and Commercial teams prioritize closure before the demo.

3. Dashboard and Data Pipeline Performance — MEDIUM / HIGH / Score 6
- Status: Active.
- Issue: Dashboard performance benchmarking has not started, and large file ingestion currently fails above approximately 200k records.
- Targets: Dashboard load time below 2 seconds for 100k records; ingestion throughput target of 500k records/min sustained.
- Impact: Meridian specifically raised concern about dashboard load times, and Q3 roadmap includes performance hardening as a key commitment.
- Mitigation owner: Derek Okafor for dashboard benchmark; Amir Hassan for ingestion crash.
- Leadership view: This risk is under-owned operationally because Derek is also a key-person dependency. Consider additional technical support.

4. UAT Timeline Slippage — HIGH / MEDIUM / Score 6
- Status: Active.
- Issue: UAT has already been extended by 1 week and is now scheduled for 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28.
- Impact: Slippage greater than 2 weeks may push launch into Q4 and trigger Meridian contract Section 7.1 penalty review.
- Mitigation owner: Sarah Chen.
- Leadership view: Protect UAT entry criteria and actively manage Meridian IT dependencies.

5. Meridian IT Environment Access Delays — MEDIUM / MEDIUM / Score 4
- Status: Active.
- Issue: UAT environment provisioning is blocked by Meridian IT access and pending infrastructure inputs.
- Meridian commitment: IT infrastructure diagram due from Priya Kapoor by 2026-07-12; UAT environment access expected by 2026-07-19.
- Mitigation owner: Nadia Petrov.
- Leadership view: Escalate quickly if Meridian misses the 2026-07-12 infrastructure diagram commitment.

6. Key-Person Dependency — LOW / HIGH / Score 3
- Status: Monitored.
- Issue: Derek Okafor is a key dependency for technical leadership and performance benchmarking; no backup tech lead has been identified.
- Leadership view: Assign backup technical ownership before August UAT and performance hardening.

7. Scope Creep — LOW / MEDIUM / Score 2
- Status: Monitored.
- Issue: Three informal Meridian feature requests have been logged but not escalated.
- Q3 proposed additions requiring change control approval include scheduled ingestion jobs, email alerting for pipeline failures, and a self-service mapping rule editor.
- Leadership view: Reinforce that new features require formal change control to protect launch date.

UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS

July 2026 — Complete System Integration
- Finalize data ingestion module by 2026-07-18.
- Complete client dashboard v1 by 2026-07-22.
- Complete integration test sign-off by 2026-07-25.
- Complete integration test suite setup by 2026-07-20.
- Resolve GeoSync license renewal by 2026-07-14.
- Receive Meridian clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Receive Meridian IT infrastructure diagram by 2026-07-12.
- Secure UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

July 25, 2026 — Meridian Product Demo
Confirmed requirements:
- Demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Demonstrate live ingestion using Meridian sample data.
- Provide end-to-end pipeline walkthrough.
- Show dashboard visualization with real data per contract Section 9.3.
- NovaBridge to send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18; owner: Sarah Chen.

August 17-28, 2026 — Meridian User Acceptance Testing
- Deploy to UAT environment.
- Support Meridian user testing sessions.
- Address UAT feedback across two budgeted sprints.
- Note: UAT period has already been extended by 1 week.

August-September 2026 — Performance Hardening
- Achieve dashboard load time below 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Achieve ingestion throughput of 500k records/min sustained.
- Conduct external security penetration test; vendor TBD.

September 2026 — Launch Preparation
- Complete production environment setup.
- Finalize runbook documentation.
- Finalize 30-day post-launch hypercare plan.
- Conduct final client training sessions.
- Target launch date remains 2026-10-01.

FINANCIAL HEALTH
- Q2 budget: $302,000.
- Q2 actual: $322,000.
- Q2 variance: +$20,000 over budget.
- Primary overrun drivers: internal engineering overrun of $14,200 due to an unplanned sprint in May; subcontractor overrun of $6,800 for GeoSync integration scoping; cloud infrastructure overrun of $3,500 due to early load testing environment setup.
- Offsets: QA/testing was $1,900 under budget; travel/client meetings were $2,600 under budget.
- Projected additional Q3 overrun exposure: $12,000 for GeoSync license if not renewed before expiry; $15,000 for UAT extension.
- Estimated H2 projected overrun: $47,000.
- Contract note: Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group.
- Upcoming finance commitments: CFO review scheduled for 2026-07-18; budget reconciliation plan due to CFO by 2026-07-20.

ISSUES REQUIRING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION

1. Escalate Meridian Data and Environment Dependencies
Leadership should ensure Meridian delivers the clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 and the IT infrastructure diagram by 2026-07-12. These two inputs directly affect ingestion completion, UAT environment readiness, and demo credibility.

2. Fast-Track GeoSync License Resolution
GeoSync licensing must be closed by 2026-07-14 to protect the 2026-07-25 demo. Leadership may need to prioritize Legal review and approve commercial terms quickly if renewal terms are within acceptable limits.

3. Address Performance and Scalability Before Demo
The current memory overflow at approximately 210k records and the not-yet-started dashboard benchmark are significant risks, especially because Meridian has raised explicit concern about dashboard load times. Leadership should consider allocating an additional senior engineer or performance specialist to support Derek Okafor and Amir Hassan immediately.

4. Confirm UAT Readiness and Entry Criteria
The UAT window has already moved to 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-28. Leadership should require a clear UAT readiness checkpoint before the end of July covering environment availability, test data, defect thresholds, support model, and Meridian stakeholder availability.

5. Manage Budget Overrun Exposure
Q2 is already $20,000 over budget, with H2 overrun exposure estimated at $47,000. Since internal overruns cannot be passed to Meridian, leadership should review the reconciliation plan before the 2026-07-20 CFO deadline and decide whether contingency or scope tradeoffs are required.

6. Enforce Change Control for New Feature Requests
Proposed Q3 additions should not enter delivery without formal approval. Leadership should reinforce change control with Meridian to avoid jeopardizing UAT, performance hardening, and the 2026-10-01 launch date.

7. Reduce Key-Person Risk
Derek Okafor is central to technical leadership and performance benchmarking, yet no backup tech lead is identified. Leadership should nominate a backup technical owner before the project enters UAT.

RECOMMENDED LEADERSHIP DECISIONS / ACTIONS THIS WEEK
- Confirm escalation path with Meridian leadership if the infrastructure diagram is not received by 2026-07-12.
- Require daily status updates on clean sample data delivery through 2026-07-15.
- Direct Legal and Raj Patel to close GeoSync renewal by 2026-07-14 or provide an approved fallback plan.
- Assign an additional senior engineer or performance specialist to support ingestion scalability and dashboard benchmark work by 2026-07-14.
- Request a demo readiness review no later than 2026-07-18, covering live ingestion, dashboard readiness, GeoSync license status, and open high-severity defects.
- Review the budget reconciliation plan before the CFO deadline of 2026-07-20.
- Name a backup technical lead for Derek Okafor before August UAT.

OVERALL HEALTH ASSESSMENT
Current status is ON TRACK, but with meaningful execution risk concentrated in the next two weeks. The project can still meet the 2026-07-25 demo, August UAT, and 2026-10-01 launch targets if Meridian dependencies, GeoSync licensing, and performance defects are resolved promptly. Leadership attention is warranted now to prevent schedule compression from becoming launch slippage.

content.text: Done — I prepared the comprehensive project health briefing and saved it as `project_health_briefing.txt`.
